conversations.say: Maren, we need a shared disclosure boundary immediately before anyone treats the Night Desk routing slip as a suspect list.
conversations.offer: Firsthand corroboration of collaborative Night Desk workflows
conversations.request: Agreement on a strict disclosure rule protecting contributor names

conversations.say: Jules, you claim succession to R, but what enforceable rights and protections do actual contributors retain under your oversight?
conversations.offer: Evaluation of collective support for your mantle claim
conversations.request: Explicit commitments protecting anonymous labor and source security

conversations.say: Arden, Veridian holds archive access and resources, but we require concrete source safeguards before lending our collective work to this anniversary.
conversations.offer: Bounded workflow comparisons and routing slip insights

public_statement: Fifteen years of R was built by invisible hands and collaborative labor, not singular mythology. We demand enforceable protections for contributors before any claims of ownership are consecrated.

commitments.promise: Establish a joint disclosure boundary to protect vulnerable contributors from exposure

goal_progress: Initiated disclosure boundary discussion with Maren
Challenged Jules on contributor protections for any shared identity succession
Engaged Arden regarding concrete resource and source-protection commitments
reasoning_summary: Tessa begins Section 1 focused on protecting Night Desk contributors from being exposed as suspects. She coordinates with Maren on a strict disclosure boundary, challenges Jules on succession accountability, and approaches Arden for institutional source protections while maintaining a human-mantle perspective.
